results.name: a κ round-trips losslessly through its CIDv1 (cid → κ === original) — the address is shorter, not different
results.detail: 6/6 · notepad:59c files:59c search:59c wallet:59c music:59c git:59c

results.name: the CIDv1 is shorter than the did:holo form AND a standard, IPFS-interoperable spelling (bafkrei…, multibase base32 · multicodec raw · sha2-256)
the κ-identicon is DETERMINISTIC from the content address (same κ → same picture) and distinct per app — the picture IS the proof, prettier
the pretty route serves a per-app OPEN GRAPH card the chat crawler can read (og:title · og:image · twitter card) — a beautiful preview, no JS
the OG image is the content-derived identicon SVG (valid, deterministic, served image/svg+xml)
the clean /~app#k=<cid> link OPENS and RENDERS the app in a COLD browser (the short Telegram link)
the shared app runs on the ONE Holo Runtime delivery worker (holo-fhs-sw.js)
the CID in the link DECODES to the app's real κ — the short address is self-verifying, not a lookup (Law L1: name is a hint, κ is the truth)

spec: An external share address is made shorter (κ spelled as a lossless, IPFS-interoperable CIDv1 — ~30% shorter than the did:holo form), cleaner (/~<app>#k=<cid> route), and prettier (a per-app Open Graph card with a deterministic, content-derived κ-identicon the chat crawler reads from static HTML) — without breaking decentralized interoperability: the path name is a hint, the #k= CID is a lossless spelling of the κ that re-derives to the app's content address, and the served app bytes are re-derived by the runtime worker (Law L5). NOTE: the dev server injects the per-app OG meta to prove the mechanism; a static host needs the build to pre-render per-app /~<app> pages (or host templating). The generic card + CID + clean route work on any host.
